title: Outdoor Cabana Backyard Retreat
summary: A permanent gable-roofed outdoor lounge platform — notched 4x4 posts and rafters, a raised 2x6 deck, built floor-first with the framing landing directly on it.
description:
This is a genuine small structure, not furniture: a raised deck built first and leveled into its permanent position, with notched 4x4 corner posts and a lapped gable roof frame built up from that deck. Building the floor first and placing it in its final, leveled position before framing the walls is a deliberate sequencing choice — trying to level a fully-framed structure afterward is far harder than leveling a bare deck.

Every leg notch is cut before assembly, and the posts land on the deck framing using real structural hardware — Simpson Strong-Tie post connectors, or 5" lag screws, or pocket holes as an alternative — not screws driven straight through end grain. The gable roof's peak is a lapped 4x4 joint, notched on both mating pieces so they nest together rather than butting, which is what gives the roof peak real strength without additional hardware.

**This is a permanent outdoor structure and needs to be treated like one.** It requires a level, prepared site with concrete pier blocks (or an equivalent footing) rated to carry the structure's full weight and snow/wind loading for your area, and depending on size and permanence, it may require a building permit in your jurisdiction — check local code before starting, not after the posts are set.

materials.name: Concrete pier blocks (or equivalent footing)
materials.note: Sized to your local frost line and load requirements — verify with local code.

cutList.note: One end cut at 45°, longest point measurement.
One end cut at 45°, longest point measurement.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two floor frame, outer at 89", seven floor joists at 89", two header beams at 77-1/2", three cross beams at 70-1/2" and seven rafters at 89" from the 2x4, 8 ft, ground-contact rated. Cut two floor rim boards at 89" and two ridge beams at 89" from the 2x8, 8 ft, ground-contact rated. Cut two corner posts at 93", four gable rafters, short side at 37-1/2" and four gable rafters, long side at 58-1/4" from the 4x4, 8 ft, ground-contact rated. Cut 13 decking boards at 96" and three roof cross braces at 70-1/2" from the 2x6, 8 ft, ground-contact rated. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to 21 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft, ground-contact rated, four 8 ft 2x8, 8 ft, ground-contact rated, six 8 ft 4x4, 8 ft, ground-contact rated and 16 8 ft 2x6, 8 ft, ground-contact rated. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build and level the floor
steps.body: Build the floor using pocket holes for the inner floor joists and 3" screws for the outer rim boards. Adjust for square by comparing diagonals. Place the floor in its final position on the prepared concrete pier blocks and level and bolt it down before continuing.

steps.title: Notch and set the posts
steps.body: Notch all the leg tops as shown in the reference build before you stand the posts. Lay out each notch with a square and pencil on all faces, saw the cheeks with the circular saw set to depth, and clean out the waste with the jigsaw. Stand the posts on the floor frame and fasten them with the Simpson Strong-Tie post connectors from the materials list. If your local code or chosen post base calls for a different fastening method, follow that hardware's instructions and source its specified fasteners separately; the listed materials do not include lag screws. Plumb every post in both directions and brace it temporarily with scrap before you move on.

steps.title: Build and set the gable roof frame
steps.body: Notch the main gable roof 4x4 pieces as shown, then set them together, lapping the joints at the peak and at the legs. Use 3" screws on the insides and outsides to join.

steps.title: Deck the floor
steps.body: Add the decking boards with 1/2" spacing between them, screwed down with 3" screws.

steps.title: Attach the rafters and finish
steps.body: Attach the rafters and ridge beams to the 4x4 ends with pocket holes. Sand any rough edges and apply an exterior finish rated for permanent outdoor exposure.

images.alt: Outdoor gable-roofed cabana lounge structure on a raised deck
